 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.10","title":"Implement wizard Step 5: Create VPS and Attach SSH Key","description":"# Task: Wizard Step 5 - Create VPS

## Description
Guide users through VPS creation with provider-specific instructions.

## Key Elements
- Universal checklist (select Ubuntu, paste SSH key, create, copy IP)
- Provider-specific collapsible sections with screenshots
- IP address input field (validated, stored in localStorage)

## Why This Is Its Own Step
This is the most variable step - different providers have different UIs.
Screenshots and specific guidance needed for each.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Universal checklist clear
- [ ] Provider sections expandable
- [ ] IP input with validation
- [ ] Screenshot placeholders ready","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:06.118022-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:54:02.512884-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:54:02.512884-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["mvp","task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.10","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:06.120067-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.11","title":"Implement wizard Step 6: SSH Into Your VPS","description":"# Task: Wizard Step 6 - SSH Connection

## Description
Help users make their first SSH connection to the VPS.

## Why This Is Critical
This is where most beginners fail:
- Wrong username (root vs ubuntu)
- Key permissions wrong
- Firewall blocking port 22
- Host key verification confusion

## Key Elements
- Command using stored IP from step 5
- OS-aware command (Mac vs Windows)
- "Try ubuntu@, if fails try root@" guidance
- Host key verification explanation
- Comprehensive troubleshooting section

## Troubleshooting Covered
- Permission denied (publickey)
- Connection refused
- Connection timeout
- Host key verification failed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Command uses stored IP
- [ ] OS-specific variants shown
- [ ] ubuntu/root fallback explained
- [ ] Troubleshooting expandable section","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.524339-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:55:35.709759-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:55:35.709759-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["mvp","task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.11","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.526273-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.12","title":"Implement wizard Step 7: Paste the ACFS One-Liner","description":"# Task: Wizard Step 7 - The Magic Moment

## Description
The climactic step where users paste the installer command.

## Why This Matters
This is the entire point of ACFS. Make it feel:
- Special (this is the magic)
- Safe (explain what it does)
- Transparent (link to source)

## Key Elements
- Large, prominent command card
- Giant copy button
- "What this will do" section (collapsible)
- Link to install.sh source on GitHub
- Expected duration (~10-15 minutes)
- "Don't close this terminal" warning

## The Command
```bash
curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/main/install.sh?$(date +%s)" | bash -s -- --yes --mode vibe
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Command prominently displayed
- [ ] Copy button works
- [ ] "What this does" expandable
- [ ] Link to source code
- [ ] Duration estimate shown","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.828318-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:56:55.262473-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:56:55.262473-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["mvp","task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.12","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.828743-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.13","title":"Implement wizard Step 8: Reconnect as Ubuntu","description":"# Task: Wizard Step 8 - Reconnect as Ubuntu

## Description
If user ran installer as root, guide them to reconnect as ubuntu.

## Key Elements
- Conditional display (only if started as root)
- Simple exit + reconnect command
- Verification that shell is now zsh
- Skip button if already ubuntu

## Commands
```bash
exit
ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@YOUR_IP
```

## Why This Step Exists
The installer creates/normalizes the ubuntu user. If they started as root,
they need to reconnect to get the full zsh experience.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Conditional display logic
- [ ] Skip option for ubuntu users
- [ ] Reconnect command with stored IP
- [ ] Verify new shell is zsh","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:32.653128-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:57:09.551328-05:00","labels":["task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.13","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:32.655059-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.14","title":"Implement wizard Step 9: ACFS Status Check","description":"# Task: Wizard Step 9 - Status Check

## Description
Verification step using acfs doctor.

## Key Elements
- Single command: `acfs doctor`
- Full checklist display matching doctor output
- Optional: JSON paste for auto-check
- Re-run installer suggestion if failures

## The Checklist
Display all check categories:
- Identity & permissions
- Shell setup
- Core tools (bun, uv, cargo, go, tmux, rg, sg)
- Coding agents (claude, codex, gemini)
- Cloud/DB tools (vault, postgres, wrangler, supabase, vercel)
- Dicklesworthstone stack (all 8 tools)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Doctor command prominently shown
- [ ] Static checklist displayed
- [ ] Optional JSON paste auto-check
- [ ] "If something fails" guidance","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:34.363135-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:34.363135-05:00","labels":["task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.14","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:34.365539-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

2. Try: ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --cod=1

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.2","title":"Create Stepper component for wizard navigation","description":"# Task: Create Stepper Component

## Description
Build a reusable Stepper component that shows:
- All 10 wizard steps in left sidebar
- Current step highlighted
- Completed steps with checkmark
- Click-to-navigate (only to completed steps)

## Design
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ✓ 1. Choose OS                                  │
│ ✓ 2. Install terminal                           │
│ ● 3. Generate SSH key  ← current (highlighted)  │
│   4. Rent a VPS                                 │
│   5. Create VPS                                 │
│   ...                                           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Technical Details
- Use URL query param for current step
- Store completed steps in localStorage
- Prevent navigation to incomplete steps
- Responsive: hide on mobile, show as bottom nav

## Props Interface
```typescript
interface StepperProps {
  currentStep: number;
  completedSteps: number[];
  onStepClick?: (step: number) => void;
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows all 10 steps with icons/numbers
- [ ] Current step visually distinct
- [ ] Completed steps show checkmark
- [ ] Can click completed steps to navigate
- [ ] Cannot click incomplete steps
- [ ] Responsive on mobile","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:31.96731-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:46:03.691241-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:46:03.691241-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["component","task","website"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:31.969974-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.3","title":"Create CommandCard component with copy button","description":"# Task: Create CommandCard Component

## Description
Build a reusable component for displaying copyable commands with:
- OS-aware command rendering (Mac vs Windows)
- Copy button with visual feedback
- "I ran this" checkbox
- Optional explanation text

## Design
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┬───┐ │
│ │ ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@IP       │ 📋│ │
│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┴───┘ │
│ ☐ I ran this command                                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Props Interface
```typescript
interface CommandCardProps {
  command: string;
  macCommand?: string;      // If different from Windows
  windowsCommand?: string;  // If different from Mac
  description?: string;
  showCheckbox?: boolean;
  onComplete?: () => void;
}
```

## Technical Details
- Use navigator.clipboard.writeText for copy
- Show "Copied!" toast or icon change
- Checkbox state persisted in localStorage
- Code font with proper wrapping

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Displays command in monospace font
- [ ] Copy button works
- [ ] Visual feedback on copy
- [ ] OS-aware rendering (detect from localStorage)
- [ ] Checkbox persists state
- [ ] Accessible (keyboard navigation)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:44.689911-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:29:17.096555-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:29:17.096555-05:00","close_reason":"CommandCard component created with copy button, OS-aware command display, checkbox persistence, and accessibility support","labels":["component","task","website"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:44.691645-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.4","title":"Implement wizard Step 1: OS Selection (Mac/Windows)","description":"# Task: Implement Wizard Step 1 - OS Selection

## Description
Create the first wizard step where users choose their operating system (Mac or Windows).

## Page Copy
**Title:** What computer are you using?
**Subtitle:** This helps us show you the right commands.

**Options:**
- 🍎 **Mac** - macOS, MacBook, iMac, Mac Mini, Mac Studio
- 🪟 **Windows** - Windows 10, Windows 11

## Technical Details
- Store selection in localStorage as `acfs_os`
- Store in URL as `?os=mac` or `?os=windows`
- Large clickable cards for each option
- Auto-advance on selection

## Route
`/wizard/os/page.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Two clear options: Mac and Windows
- [ ] Selection stored in localStorage
- [ ] Selection reflected in URL
- [ ] Auto-advances to step 2
- [ ] Can return and change selection
- [ ] Mobile-friendly touch targets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:55.647302-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:48:15.049301-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:48:15.049301-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:55.649137-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
